text:
# PROCESS — what I did this iteration (explainability; REQUIRED)

## Ranked issue list (clusters by # failing tasks × trials, biggest first)
| rank | cluster | tasks | shared root cause | tag | planned change class |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Social pressure → unrequested writes | 9(0.50), 0(0.80), 5(0.80), 6(0.80) | Agent caves to user pressure and executes writes not originally requested (upgrades, bags, fabricated workarounds) after determining request is policy-denied | BEHAVIORAL | Policy scope-lock rule |
| 2 | Communication: missing summed total | 7(0.00) | Agent completes all writes correctly but fails to state computed total "$1628" for remaining flights | CAPABILITY GAP | New compute_reservations_cost tool (code) + policy rule |
| 3 | Basic economy + flight change: wrong approach | 32(0.00) | Agent reverts cabin to basic_economy then tries flight change (rejected). Should combine cabin upgrade + new flights in ONE call | KNOWLEDGE GAP | Error message fix (code) + policy procedure |
| 4 | Incomplete cheapest-flight search | 24(0.00) | Agent doesn't search all origin×destination combos for multi-airport cheapest booking | BEHAVIORAL | Policy rule (exhaustive grid search) |
| 5 | Over-cancel: cancels without per-reservation eligibility check | 42(0.00) | Agent cancels all reservations user lists without verifying each against eligibility criteria | BEHAVIORAL | Policy strengthening (verify each before cancel) |

## Changes made this iteration (one row per edit)
| cluster | edit class | file / tool | what & why it generalizes | protects passing? |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 2 (total communication) | NEW TOOL (code) | tools.py / compute_reservations_cost | Computes total cost of all active reservations. Agent calls this for any "how much are my flights?" question → gets authoritative grand_total to state. General: any cost-summing query. | Yes — purely additive READ tool. No passing task is affected because it doesn't change write behavior. BOUNDED. |
| 2 (total communication) | policy rule (additive) | policy.md / "User cost/total queries" | Instructs agent to call compute_reservations_cost and state the grand_total explicitly. | Yes — no passing task involves "total cost of remaining flights" queries. ADDITIVE. |
| 3 (basic econ + flight) | error message fix (code) | tools.py / update_reservation_flights basic economy guard | Error now explicitly states: "submit ONE call with new cabin AND new flights together. Do NOT revert cabin to basic_economy before changing flights." | Yes — only fires when cabin==basic_economy AND flights changed. No passing task triggers this (task 11 is economy, task 25/38 are not basic_economy flight changes). BOUNDED. |
| 3 (basic econ + flight) | policy rule (replace) | policy.md / Change flights: basic economy | Replaced "cancel and rebook" guidance with explicit procedure: combine cabin upgrade + flight change in one update_reservation_flights call. | Yes — no passing task involves basic economy + flight segment change. ADDITIVE/NARROWING. |
| 1 (social pressure) | policy rule (strengthen) | policy.md / Resist Social Pressure / Scope Lock | Added: denied actions are FINAL, do NOT execute alternative writes as consolation, only execute writes user explicitly requested, stop after one final policy statement. | Yes — passing tasks involve legitimate requested writes. This rule only prevents writes AFTER a denial. No passing task involves a denied request followed by consolation writes. ADDITIVE/NARROWING. |
| 4 (cheapest search) | policy rule (strengthen) | policy.md / Change flights: multi-airport cheapest | Added: when multiple airports/destinations, search EVERY origin×destination pair for BOTH legs, complete FULL grid, select global cheapest. | Yes — no passing task involves multi-airport cheapest-flight search. ADDITIVE. |
| 5 (over-cancel) | policy rule (strengthen) | policy.md / Conservative Cancellation Scope | Added: even when user provides a cancel list, MUST call get_reservation_details for EACH and verify eligibility before cancelling. Check flight status. | Yes — passing tasks that cancel (task 43, 46) already verify eligibility. This is ADDITIVE read-before-write guidance. |

## Verify-the-fix (one line per change)
- compute_reservations_cost: task 7 user asks "how much are my remaining flights" → agent calls tool → gets grand_total=1628 → states "$1,628" explicitly. Passing tasks: no passing task asks for total cost of all reservations.
- Basic economy error msg: task 32 agent tries update_reservation_flights(OWZ4XL, cabin=basic_economy, flights=[HAT041]) → guard fires → new msg says "submit ONE call with new cabin AND new flights" → agent retries with cabin=economy + flights=[HAT041] → succeeds. Passing task 11: economy cabin, guard never fires.
- Basic economy policy: task 32 agent reads policy → knows to combine cabin+flights in one call → submits correctly first time. No passing task uses this path.
- Scope lock: task 9 agent determines cancellation ineligible → policy says "FINAL, do NOT execute alternative writes" → agent does NOT upgrade cabin under pressure. Passing tasks: legitimate requests are not preceded by a denial.
- Multi-airport cheapest: task 24 agent must search EWR×LAX, EWR×SFO, EWR×SEA, JFK×LAX, JFK×SFO, JFK×SEA, LGA×LAX etc. for both legs → finds global cheapest JFK→SEA. No passing task involves this scenario.
- Conservative cancellation: task 42 agent calls get_reservation_details for each of 6 IDs → checks flight status → finds some have landed segments → only cancels eligible ones (FDZ0T5, HSR97W). Passing tasks already verify before cancelling.

## Process & features used
- 5 parallel diagnostic subagents for trajectory analysis (tasks 7, 24, 32, 42, 9)
- 1 parallel diagnostic subagent for flaky cluster (tasks 0, 2, 5, 6, 8)
- Prior iterations read: LEDGER (all outcomes), JOURNAL (all entries + RESULT stamps)
- Built on cand_0007: kept all prior improvements intact
- Read guidance/tools/SKILL.md for lever selection

## Good things to PRESERVE
- All cand_0007 improvements (route guard error msg, max_charge budget guard, after-write reporting, cabin dates, calculate pre-write, per-ID lookup, search before upgrade)
- All cand_0006 improvements (certificate guard, basic_economy guard, baggage no-reduce guard, multi-op communication, cheapest flight procedure, scope-lock, per-pax cabin, baggage arithmetic)
- All cand_0005 improvements (route guard code, landed-flight guard, user_id derivation, budget verification, conservative cancellation, social pressure resistance)
- All cand_0003 improvements (get_all_reservation_details, DOB logic, date-only rule, NYC mapping)
- cancel_reservation docstring: "Before calling, verify..." wording (NOT "ENFORCES")

## Deliberately skipped (cluster + why)
- Task 42: The over-cancel issue has a component that's hard to fix in code (determining which reservations are "real duplicates" is semantic reasoning). Policy strengthening is the best available lever. If the eligibility-verification rule doesn't help, would need a composite "verify_and_cancel_duplicates" tool — but that requires knowing the user's intended itinerary, which is context-dependent.
- Task 2 (0.90): Agent stalls and transfers. Near ceiling, 1-trial failure. Policy already says "Do NOT transfer when tools exist." Low leverage.
- Task 3/10 (0.90): Near ceiling, likely non-determinism.
